name: Cheerio
what_it_does: Fast, flexible jQuery-like HTML parsing and manipulation for Node.js. Cheerio loads HTML into a traversable DOM using CSS selectors ($('#id'), $('a.link').attr('href')) without a browser or headless Chrome. Used for HTML scraping, parsing HTML API responses, and server-side DOM manipulation where a headless browser is too heavy.
use_cases: Scrape structured data from HTML pages in agent data collection pipelines using jQuery-style CSS selector navigation
Parse HTML email content or API responses in agent workflows where HTML must be navigated without a full browser
Extract specific elements (tables, lists, metadata) from HTML documents using Cheerio's familiar jQuery-like API
Manipulate HTML in agent content processing pipelines — clean, sanitize, or transform HTML documents server-side
Build lightweight web scraping agents for pages that don't require JavaScript execution — faster and lighter than Puppeteer
not_for: JavaScript-rendered pages (React, Vue, Angular SPAs) — Cheerio only parses static HTML; use Puppeteer or Playwright for JS-rendered content
Browser automation (clicking, form submission, navigation) — Cheerio is a parser only, not a browser; use Playwright for browser automation
PDF or Word document parsing — use specialized PDF/DOCX parsers; Cheerio handles HTML only
best_when: Parsing static HTML documents or server-rendered pages in Node.js where jQuery-style selector navigation is familiar and a headless browser would be overkill.
avoid_when: The target pages require JavaScript execution to render content — use Puppeteer or Playwright for JS-rendered SPAs.

auth.notes: No authentication — HTML parsing library. HTTP requests (to fetch HTML) are handled separately by fetch/got/axios.

pricing.notes: Cheerio is open source and free.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local HTML parsing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Cheerio fails gracefully on malformed HTML (parses what it can). Invalid selectors throw with the selector string in the error. Missing elements return empty arrays (not null) — jQuery-style empty collection.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: HTML parsing is deterministic — same HTML input always produces same parsed structure.
known_agent_gotchas: Cheerio does NOT execute JavaScript — pages using React, Vue, or any client-side rendering will return empty or skeleton HTML; use Puppeteer for JS-rendered pages
Cheerio 1.x changed the default parser from htmlparser2 to parse5 — this changes how malformed HTML is handled; test existing scraper output after upgrading from 0.x
CSS selector behavior differs slightly from browser — some complex pseudo-selectors (:nth-child with expressions) may not work; test selectors in browser first
$('selector') returns a Cheerio object (jQuery-like) not an array — use .each() to iterate, not for..of; Array.from($('selector')) converts to array if needed
Relative URLs in href/src attributes are returned as-is — Cheerio doesn't resolve them against the base URL; construct absolute URLs manually using new URL(href, baseUrl)
Memory usage with large HTML documents (>1MB) can be significant — Cheerio builds a full DOM tree in memory; stream processing of very large HTML is not supported

security_score_components.security_notes: Local HTML parsing. XSS not applicable (server-side only). Ensure HTML from untrusted sources is sanitized before processing agent-generated outputs from Cheerio-processed content.
